response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack engineer with deep TypeScript/Node.js expertise and a broad multi-language background, but he does not meet the core mandatory requirements for this role. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2007–2008) and a bachelor thesis project, far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development required. There is no demonstrable capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain experience, and no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency. The RIDE Capital stint touches finance tangentially but involves no capital-markets systems work. The gap between his actual skill set and the job's mandatory criteria is too large to bridge.
strengths: 20+ years of professional software development with consistent delivery across diverse domains and tech stacks
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) demonstrated across multiple roles
Proven communication and knowledge-sharing ability through workshops, conference talks, and open-source contributions
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months at National Instruments in 2008 and a thesis project), nowhere near the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
Zero capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or Summit platform exposure
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work references MongoDB and MySQL in web contexts
